evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis log-loss + cosine LR exactly as briefed; color-coupled spines/ticks; directional-semantics caveat addressed; final-loss annotation added.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none both set; Arial is primary font (confirmed in SVG); DejaVu Sans falls back for math glyphs (×, 10^{-3}) because Arial lacks them — not a default, but still present in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Deep blue (#0F4D92, 8.4:1 vs white) and plum (#9A4D8E, 5.5:1 vs white); no red/green; 97.7° hue separation; redundant solid-vs-dashed encoding for colorblind safety.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,4) with tight_layout and bbox_inches=tight; log scale keeps loss curve well-spread; minor log ticks suppressed; legend placed lower-left inside axes without overlapping data.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly (colors, scale, values); directional-semantics caveat explicitly stated; legend inside axes; annotated final loss value; no obvious Reviewer-2 hooks.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 confirmed; PDF contains 22 BT text blocks with CIDFontType2/FontFile2 embedding for both ArialMT and DejaVuSans — text is fully selectable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (142 KB, 1766×1168 @ 300 dpi), figure.svg (22 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Cites specific numbers (loss 2.19→0.21 at step 199, LR ×10^{-3} from 1.0 to 0), explains color/style encoding, notes directional-semantics asymmetry, and cites data source (N=200 steps).

overall_strengths: Exemplary caption: quantitative claims, color-coupling explanation, and directional-semantics caveat all present — rare to see this level of care.
Redundant encoding (color + line style) and high-contrast palette make the figure robust for colorblind readers and greyscale print.
overall_weaknesses: DejaVu Sans appears in the PDF/SVG for math symbols (×, superscripts) because Arial lacks those glyphs; a math-capable sans-serif (e.g. Fira Sans, Source Sans) or explicit Unicode substitution would eliminate the fallback.

Contrast ratio between the two series colors is only 1.53:1 against each other (though both are high-contrast vs white); in a monochrome print the lines could be hard to distinguish without the dashed style.
